content.problemStatement: Two friends live in a small city. The city is built in the form of a circular road and skyscrapers surrounding the road. But one day the two friends quarreled with each other, and although they remained in the city, they decided to live in apartments located as far as possible from each other, or more precisely, so as to maximize the travel time between them. All the skyscrapers are arranged in a circle around the road (so you can travel in a cycle as follows: 1 <-> 2 <-> 3 <-> ... <-> n <-> 1), and the travel time between two neighbouring buildings is 1. The travel time between two adjacent floors of a skyscraper is also 1, and the friends can choose to live in any floor of the building, from the 0-th to the k-th, assuming that the skyscraper in question has k floors. Thus, the travel time from the j-th floor to ground level is j time units.
constraints: 1 ≤ n ≤ 10⁶ (sum of n over all test cases ≤ 10⁶)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a line with an integer n followed by n integers h₁ … hₙ – the heights of the skyscrapers in clockwise order.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximal possible travel time between the two friends.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The maximum travel time is achieved when the two friends live in the first and third skyscrapers, which have heights 1 and 3 respectively, and the distance between them is 2.
The maximum travel time is achieved when the two friends live in the second and seventh skyscrapers, which have heights 7 and 9 respectively, and the distance between them is 4.
The maximum travel time is achieved when the two friends live in any two adjacent skyscrapers, which have heights 1 and 1 respectively, and the distance between them is 1 and the height is 1.

solution.approach: To maximize the travel time between two friends, we need to find the maximum distance between any two points on the circular road. This can be achieved by considering the maximum height of the skyscrapers and the distance between them. We can calculate the maximum travel time by finding the maximum sum of the heights of the skyscrapers and the distance between them.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, O(T*n) total. No precomputation is done.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space used does not grow with the size of the input, only a constant amount of space is used.

code.Python3:
import sys

T = int(sys.stdin.readline())
for _ in range(T):
    n = int(sys.stdin.readline().split()[0])
    h = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
    maxTravelTime = 0
    for i in range(n):
        travelTime = h[i] + h[(i + n // 2) % n] + n // 2
        maxTravelTime = max(travelTime, maxTravelTime)
    print(maxTravelTime)
